'''Shumari''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Parmel sector/Legends|Parmel sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr]] was rumored to have traveled to this planet to confront the Demon Outlander who wiped out his village, otherwise known as [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

==Behind the scenes==
Originally published in Japan, stories from [[Tokyopop's Star Wars Manga|''Star Wars Manga'']] were considered to be of "fuzzy" continuity by Lucasfilm.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=980#15895005|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-11-06|time=6:03 PM}}</ref> Since then, they have been republished in English in the United Kingdom under [[Infinities]] label, which established the material in them as non-[[canon]]ical.

Author [[Daniel Wallace]] stated definitively in the article ''[[We Need to Talk About Jar Jar]]'', written for [[Star Wars Insider 131|''Star Wars Insider'' 131]] in [[February]] [[2012]], that the stories included in the ''[[Star Wars Manga: Black]]'' comic trade paperback are non-canon.<ref name="Insider 131">{{InsiderCite|131|We Need to Talk About Jar Jar}}</ref> Despite this, however, the events of the comic were briefly alluded to later in the August 22, 2013 Blog [[Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire]] Part 2, where it mentioned that one of the aliens aligned with the Galactic Empire, KkH'Oar'Rrhr, had traveled to the planet with the hopes of slaying the "Demon Outlander" (Darth Vader).

The planet [[Shumari]] has been later referenced in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', which included the [[Shumari system/Legends|Shumari system]] in its appendix. However, according to [[Jason Fry]], "its inclusion shouldn't be taken as a statement of canonicity for anything except the geography."<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/29648348|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2009-01-04|time=7:52 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200714135715/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-72}}</ref>

===Non-canon History===
{{Noncanonstart|legends=1}}
The planet Shumari was the site of a devastating [[Battle of Shumari|battle]] in which [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] survivors of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] clashed with Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] lead by Darth Vader. While the battle raged planetside, Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] positioned above the planet subjected it to heavy [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] which, much like the [[destruction of Taris]] four millennia earlier, devastated the surface of the planet completely, rendering it uninhabitable.

The Jedi forces were eventually all eliminated but one. The Shumari native [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Tao (Sith Apprentice)|Tao]] was spared by Vader, who took him as his secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]].

Years later, in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Vader returned to Shumari with Tao, who was dying from wounds inflicted upon him by Vader in a duel. It was Vader's intention to let Tao see his home planet one last time. Suddenly Vader was attacked by a [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr|vengeful Tusken Raider]] who had survived Anakin Skywalker's massacre of the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] of the [[Valley of the Spirits]] tribe in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. The Tusken, who had sworn to hunt down Vader and kill him, had learned [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and been trained in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. Using a modified [[Gaderffii/Legends|gaffi stick]], he managed to knock Vader down.

Before the Tusken managed to deliver the final blow, Tao struggled to his feet and used his last strength to defend the Dark Lord. Tao managed to ward of the attack and kill the Tusken, thus saving Vader's life. Seconds later, the Shumari Jedi collapsed on the ground, dead from his wounds. Vader raised a monument to the Shumari Jedi which was to be the last hand-made object to be made on the planet.
{{Noncanonend}}
